2606.26875.title: Information-Aware KV Cache Compression for Long Reasoning
2606.26875.paper_summary: Reasoning capability has advanced rapidly in large language models (LLMs), leading to an increasing size of key-value (KV) cache in both prefilling and decoding stages. Existing KV cache compression methods mainly rely on attention weights to estimate token importance. While attention effectively captures contextual relevance, it overlooks complementary information-theoretic signals related to predictive uncertainty and token informativeness. In this paper, we revisit token importance from a forward-looking perspective and introduce \textit{Forward Influence}, a metric that measures how compressed tokens affect future contexts. Our analysis reveals that tokens selected by attention scores mainly influence nearby contexts, whereas tokens associated with high predictive uncertainty exhibit substantially stronger influence on distant future contexts. Based on the observation, we propose \textbf{InfoKV}, an entropy-aware KV cache compression framework that incorporates information-theoretic signals. It combines token-level predictive uncertainty with layer-wise representation evolution and integrates the resulting entropy scores with attention scores during reasoning. Experiments on long-context reasoning benchmarks with Llama-3.1, Llama-3.2, and DeepSeek-R1 demonstrate that InfoKV consistently outperforms existing attention-based KV compression methods in both long prefilling and decoding scenarios.
2606.26875.paper_summary_zh: 推理能力在大型語言模型（LLMs）中迅速提升，導致在預填充和解碼階段中關鍵值（KV）快取的大小不斷增加。現有的KV快取壓縮方法主要依賴注意力權重來估計標記的重要性。雖然注意力有效捕捉上下文相關性，但它忽略了與預測不確定性和標記信息性相關的互補信息理論信號。在本文中，我們從前瞻性的角度重新審視標記的重要性，並引入\textit{Forward Influence}，這是一個衡量壓縮標記如何影響未來上下文的指標。我們的分析顯示，根據注意力分數選擇的標記主要影響附近的上下文，而與高預測不確定性相關的標記對遙遠的未來上下文則表現出更強的影響力。基於這一觀察，我們提出了\textbf{InfoKV}，這是一個考慮熵的KV快取壓縮框架，納入了信息理論信號。它將標記級預測不確定性與層級表示演變相結合，並在推理過程中將結果熵分數與注意力分數整合。在使用Llama-3.1、Llama-3.2和DeepSeek-R1的長上下文推理基準上進行的實驗顯示，InfoKV在長預填充和解碼場景中始終優於現有的基於注意力的KV壓縮方法。
2606.26875.author: Jushi Kai et.al.
2606.26875.authors: Jushi Kai, Zhuiri Xiao, Alexandra Birch, Zhouhan Lin
